actual_mixed_order_5_projection.meaning: M^[5] means the z^5 coefficient/projection of the full mixed input
actual_mixed_order_5_projection.reason: r2,r4,r6 are scalar grades 0,2,4 by the same-point rule

homogeneous_common_scalar_grade.reason: common scalar Gr_5 uses r7, but r7 has no z5 mixed support

parity_corrected_nonhomogeneous_source_p.meaning: declare Gr_5^M to include exactly the source combination producing [z^5]M
parity_corrected_nonhomogeneous_source_p.required_new_theorem: separate mixed-output grade projection and proof that using r2,r4,r6 in M^[5] does not consume or duplicate grade 0,2,4 same-point/Frechet sectors

decision.next_gate_reason: these are the next Y-containing cubic families; they require second-Frechet tables plus the same mixed-input convention

scope: ordinary-z, pre-Phi_K compatibility of M^[5] with Lambda^[1]

source_facts.same_point_grade_rule: r^(k)(m) first appears in delta G^lin at z^(k-2)
